title: Victorious Incumbents Vow Change For CA
author: David Joachim
abstract: InformationWeek reports the aftermath of the Sam Wyly proxy fight against Computer Associates' incumbent board (Charles Wang/Sanjay Kumar). Aberdeen Group analyst Peter Kastner argues CA's customer-facing changes were driven by a 'standing pejorative that CA screws its customers' rather than by Wyly pressure — CA was already 'tweaking sales and support organization' continuously. Customer Andrew Winer of Myers Industries voiced relief that splitting the company was averted. Date inferred from Wyly post-fight context [REVIEW].
importance_rationale: Documents the customer-perception crisis that defined CA in the 2000s and presaged the 2004 accounting scandal forced-out of Sanjay Kumar.
relevance_rationale: CA was acquired by Broadcom (2018); the proxy fight's specifics are historical.
prescience_rationale: Kastner's framing that CA's customer issues predated and outlasted the proxy fight proved correct — the company never escaped the reputation, ultimately exiting via Broadcom acquisition in 2018.
